title: azalea viscosa var. hispida
intro: Azalea viscosa var. hispida, also known as sticky azalea, is a plant species in the heath family (Ericaceae) native to the southeastern United States. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Azalea viscosa var. hispida include its ability to soothe and calm the skin, reduce inflammation, and provide relief from pain and discomfort. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally to leverage its medicinal properties.
